name: realtor
title: Real estate transactions
description: For an agent running listings and escrows off an Asana transaction checklist. Documents that arrive by email file themselves and check off the matching task; deadlines produce draft client emails.
whenToOffer: The user mentions listings, escrow, transactions, closings, contingencies, MLS, disclosures, or being a real-estate agent.

skills.name: transaction-import
skills.summary: Finds existing Asana transaction projects and registers them all, reading details from each project's Transaction Info task.

skills.name: transaction-setup
skills.summary: One-step new deal: document folder, Asana link, registry block.

skills.name: transaction-autopilot
skills.summary: The "catch me up" runner. Evaluates the playbook across every active deal.

riskGate.prompt: It reads documents and email that arrived from outside **and** changes your Asana project on your behalf. A malicious or mislabeled document could try to steer those changes. It only completes tasks, comments, and adjusts dates in the deals you list; it logs every change; it never deletes or sends email. Proceed?

skills.name: transaction-fetch
skills.summary: Pulls document attachments from Gmail into the deal folder. Needs a Gmail MCP server that can download attachments.

riskGate.prompt: It reads your email and saves attachments into your deal folders, then labels those messages as processed. It never sends, replies to, forwards, or deletes mail. Proceed?

skills.name: transaction-briefing
skills.summary: One-page daily brief per deal from Asana. Read-only.

skills.name: transaction-emailer
skills.summary: Milestone client emails in the agent's voice, always cc'ing them. Never sends.

skills.name: transaction-intake
skills.summary: Files documents, updates Asana, queues follow-ups.

firstRun.why: you almost certainly have deals running in Asana already, and this registers them all at once instead of one at a time
firstRun.skipIf: the deal registry already lists deals

memory.note: Ships ready to use. Never ask the user to configure it — they change rules by talking.
The deal registry. Write a commented template block; never invent a deal.
The action ledger. Every rule fires once per trigger; this is what remembers.

connectors.name: google-drive
connectors.why: document folders; local folders work without it

extensions.name: gmail-attachments
extensions.why: downloads document attachments out of Gmail — no built-in connector can do this, including Google's own
extensions.alternativeTo: documents dropped into the deal folder by hand, or by a cloud ingest service

extensions.name: outlook-attachments
extensions.why: same job for Outlook / Microsoft 365 mailboxes
extensions.alternativeTo: documents dropped into the deal folder by hand, or by a cloud ingest service

schedules.why: files documents and drafts due reminders; safe at this cadence because it never acts twice on the same thing
the day's read before you start
